metadata.description: Test cases for let-binding hoisting transformations

literal.string: nested let inside lambda: binding hoisted with lambda capture
type application: nested let outside lambda CAN be hoisted
no polymorphic bindings: simple let unchanged
no polymorphic bindings: multiple monomorphic bindings
single polymorphic binding: already at top level
polymorphic binding inside lambda: no capture
polymorphic binding captures lambda variable: wrapped in lambda
polymorphic binding captures multiple lambda variables
polymorphic binding captures some but not all lambda variables
polymorphic binding captures both lambda-bound and let-bound variables
sibling polymorphic bindings inside lambda: one calls the other
sibling polymorphic bindings inside lambda: h passes its own args to g
no name collision: distinct names after unshadowing
nested polymorphic binding calls enclosing polymorphic binding
polymorphic binding captures monomorphic sibling in same let
nested lets: poly binding references poly sibling from outer let
polymorphic binding with pair: type applications preserved
monomorphic binding captures type vars: replacement includes type applications
nested function types: all type variables must be declared
type variable in return position only
type variables in deeply nested generics
multiple bindings with overlapping type variable names
short type variable names are treated as type parameters
numbered type variables like t0 t1 t2
Test cases for let-binding hoisting transformations
